title: Compute a stable explicit step size
description: Derive the maximum stable step for an explicit solver from the system's fastest time constant.
body: Use when setting a fixed step for an explicit integrator on a known system. Do not exceed the stability limit, since the solution diverges abruptly beyond it. Actions: find the fastest eigenvalue or time constant, apply the solver's stability factor to get the maximum step, and verify the solution stays bounded at that step. Validation signals include a bounded trajectory at the computed step and divergence appearing just beyond it. Risk boundary: running at the exact limit is fragile against parameter change, so flag a step at the boundary and back off with a safety margin.
